scenarios.title: Computer Vision Detection and Segmentation Workflows
scenarios.when_to_read: Object detection, instance/keypoint/panoptic/semantic segmentation, Detectron2 model zoo, datasets, training, inference, visualization, and export tasks.
scenarios.role: Guides Detectron2 configuration, datasets, training/evaluation, inference/visualization, deployment export, and extension/project workflows.
scenarios.read_when: The request names Detectron2, Detectron, model_zoo, DefaultPredictor, DefaultTrainer, DatasetCatalog, MetadataCatalog, Instances, Boxes, Mask R-CNN, Faster R-CNN, RetinaNet, PointRend, DeepLab, DensePose, ViTDet, TorchScript export, or COCO/LVIS/Cityscapes detection workflows.
scenarios.best_for: Using Detectron2 APIs and standard workflows for configs/model zoo, custom datasets, training/evaluation, inference/visualization, deployment export, and custom model/project extension.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is only generic PyTorch, OpenCV image processing, or a different computer vision framework with no Detectron2-specific APIs, configs, data formats, or errors.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `detectron2` for Detectron2-specific detection/segmentation work. For pure image preprocessing or general PyTorch model code, prefer a generic PyTorch/OpenCV skill unless the request depends on Detectron2 configs, catalogs, model zoo, trainers, structures, evaluators, or export APIs.

scenarios.title: Training and Fine-Tuning Workflows
scenarios.when_to_read: Training, fine-tuning, evaluation, distributed launch, checkpoints, hooks, solver schedules, and dataset-backed model adaptation.
scenarios.role: Provides Detectron2-specific training/evaluation command shapes, trainer/evaluator/checkpoint APIs, and troubleshooting for custom datasets and model zoo configs.
scenarios.read_when: The request mentions Detectron2 training, DefaultTrainer, SimpleTrainer, launch, train/eval drivers, COCOEvaluator, inference_on_dataset, DetectionCheckpointer, SOLVER settings, MODEL.ROI_HEADS.NUM_CLASSES, or fine-tuning a model-zoo config.
scenarios.best_for: Detectron2 training/evaluation setup, one-GPU adaptation of model-zoo recipes, custom evaluator/checkpoint planning, and diagnosing registered-dataset or checkpoint failures.
scenarios.avoid_when: The training task uses a non-Detectron2 framework, does not use Detectron2 data/config/model APIs, or is only about generic optimizer math.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `detectron2` when training/evaluation depends on Detectron2 config keys, data catalogs, trainers, evaluators, or model zoo checkpoints. Pair the training route with data/config sub-skills when custom datasets or config overrides are involved.

scenarios.title: Model Deployment and Export Workflows
scenarios.when_to_read: TorchScript, tracing, scripting, Caffe2/ONNX export, model analysis, benchmark planning, and deployment diagnostics for ML models.
scenarios.role: Guides Detectron2 deployment export choices, dry-run export/analysis command construction, optional dependency checks, and model-structure limitations.
scenarios.read_when: The request mentions Detectron2 export_model, TorchScript, TracingAdapter, scripting_with_instances, dump_torchscript_IR, Caffe2Tracer, ONNX export, FLOP/activation/parameter analysis, or benchmark.py in a Detectron2 context.
scenarios.best_for: Detectron2 model export planning, TorchScript method selection, optional Caffe2/ONNX caveats, and safe analysis/benchmark command previews.
scenarios.avoid_when: The deployment task targets a generic PyTorch model without Detectron2 structures or export APIs, or requires a production ONNX/TensorRT pipeline outside Detectron2's supported export surface.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `detectron2` for export/deployment tasks involving Detectron2 model inputs, `Instances`, official model families, or Detectron2 export helpers; use a generic deployment skill for unrelated model serving stacks.
